steps.title: Calculate Jupiter's weight from the given percentage
steps.content:
Weight on Jupiter is 252.8% of weight on Earth. Convert the percentage to a decimal and multiply:
$$\text{Weight on Jupiter} = 2.528 \times 170 = 429.76 \text{ pounds}$$

steps.title: Find x: Neptune weight as a percentage of Jupiter weight
steps.content:
Set up the equation: Neptune weight = x% of Jupiter weight.
$$186.32 = \frac{x}{100} \times 429.76$$
Solve for x by multiplying both sides by 100 and dividing by 429.76:
$$x = \frac{186.32}{429.76} \times 100 = 0.4335 \times 100 = 43.35$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
